marketName: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment Manufacturing

assessments.rationale: Hydronic circuits in heat pumps and chillers use water or water-glycol — exact media match.
Heating mode 35-55 degC (standard) to 65 degC (high-temp HP). Cooling mode 5-12 degC. Well within 90 degC limit.
Hydronic circuits 1-4 bar typical. Safety relief valves cap at 3-6 bar. Well within 10 bar.
Heat pump/chiller hydronic flows 5-40 l/min. Minimum detectable 4.2 l/min (DN20) or 0.25 l/min (DN14) — below operating range.
Closed-loop systems de-aerated during factory commissioning. Properly filled: <0.5% air content.
Closed-loop pressurized circuits fully filled during manufacturing. Partially filled = defect caught at performance testing.
OEM controls internal piping layout. Sensor placement designed into hydraulic module from the start. Solved constraint in OEM-integrated design.
Moderate temperature range (5-65 degC) with gradual changes. NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%.
